sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel data documented for this NES wrestling game

special_mechanics.name: Special Moves
special_mechanics.description: Activated after collecting energy ball thrown by trainer, B button executes special move

special_mechanics.name: Rope Rebound
special_mechanics.description: Flying body attack via jumping into ropes and rebounding

special_mechanics.name: Lariat/Clothesline
special_mechanics.description: Shove enemy into ropes then press A button

special_mechanics.name: Back Drop/Suplex
special_mechanics.description: Press B button when positioned close behind enemy

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Qualitative data only. Wikipedia article provides gameplay mechanics overview but contains no documented numerical physics parameters. Game described as 'primitive' even for early NES standards. Wrestling game with energy ball collection system affecting special move availability. Speed level increases as rounds progress (rounds 1-99 have reduced time per round at 10:00, while early rounds have 30:00). Game uses 8-bit round counter resetting at 256 rounds.
